name: Table
summary: A Table is a series of rows and columns that holds data.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a Layout::Table with a specified size, and a specified number of rows and columns.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a Layout::Table with a specified size, and a specified number of rows and columns.

throws.when: if rows is less than 1
if columns is less than 1
if bounds is zero size

methods.summary: The #[] method returns the Layout::TableCell at the specified row and column.
methods.remarks: The #[] method returns the Layout::TableCell at the specified row and column.

throws.when: if row_index or column_index are not a valid indices for the Layout::Table

methods.name: dimensions
methods.summary: The #dimensions method returns the number of rows and columns in a Layout::Table.
methods.remarks: The #dimensions method returns the number of rows and columns in a Layout::Table.

returns.doc: The first value is the number of rows; the second, the number of columns.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method iterates in column major order through all of the cells in the Layout::Table.
methods.remarks: The #each method iterates in column major order through all of the cells in the Layout::Table.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The #entities method creates and returns the Entities that represent the Layout::Table in its exploded form.
methods.remarks: The #entities method creates and returns the Entities that represent the Layout::Table in its exploded form.

methods.name: get_column
methods.summary: The #get_column method returns the Layout::TableColumn at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #get_column method returns the Layout::TableColumn at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table

methods.name: get_row
methods.summary: The #get_row method returns the Layout::TableRow at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #get_row method returns the Layout::TableRow at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table

methods.name: insert_column
methods.summary: The #insert_column method inserts a new column at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #insert_column method inserts a new column at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table
if the Layout::Table is on a locked Layer
if the Layout::Table is locked

methods.name: insert_row
methods.summary: The #insert_row method inserts a new row at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #insert_row method inserts a new row at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table
if the Layout::Table is on a locked Layer
if the Layout::Table is locked

methods.name: merge
methods.summary: The #merge method merges a range of cells within a Layout::Table.
methods.remarks: The #merge method merges a range of cells within a Layout::Table. Only cells which are not already merged can be merged.

throws.when: if the passed in indices are not a valid for the Layout::Table
if the Layout::Table is on a locked Layer
if the Layout::Table is locked
if the specified range of cells only spans a single cell
if the specified range of cells contains a merged cell

methods.name: remove_column
methods.summary: The #remove_column method removes the column at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #remove_column method removes the column at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table
if the Layout::Table is on a locked Layer
if the Layout::Table is locked

methods.name: remove_row
methods.summary: The #remove_row method removes the row at the specified index.
methods.remarks: The #remove_row method removes the row at the specified index.

throws.when: if index is not a valid index for the Layout::Table
if the Layout::Table is on a locked Layer
if the Layout::Table is locked
